Progress deeper each week.
Goal: full depth squat with perfect tracking.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Keep upright.
injuryNotes.knee: Final McKenzie knee step. Full squat is the goal.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Final McKenzie knee step — full functional squat.
whyForYou: When you can full squat pain-free, your knee rehab is complete.
proTip: Goblet squat position helps maintain upright torso.

name: Supine Hip Flexion (Knee to Chest)
steps: Lie on back. Pull one knee to chest.
Hold 30s. Hands behind knee.
Switch sides.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Flexion — safe for most backs.
injuryNotes.knee: Hands behind knee, not on shin.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: McKenzie Hip Step 1 — basic hip flexion ROM.
whyForYou: Foundation for hip mobility restoration.
proTip: Compare sides — the tighter side needs more time.

name: Supine Hip Rotation (IR/ER)
steps: Lie on back, knee bent to 90°.
Let knee fall INWARD (IR) — hold 15s.
Let knee fall OUTWARD (ER) — hold 15s.
Compare sides.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Supine — safe.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Assess and restore hip rotation symmetry.
whyForYou: Rotation asymmetry forces your back to compensate.
proTip: The restricted direction needs 2x the time.

name: Prone Hip Extension

progressionChain.unlockCriteria: Full hip extension without back compensation

steps: Lie face down. Squeeze one glute.
Lift that leg 3-4 inches off ground.
Hold 3s. Lower slowly.
10 reps each side.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Small ROM protects back. No arching.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Glute activation in hip extension pattern.
whyForYou: Teaches glutes to fire before back compensates.
proTip: Place hand on glute — feel it fire before the leg lifts.

name: Standing Hip Flexion Stretch

progressionChain.unlockCriteria: 30s hold both sides without back arching

steps: Staggered stance. Back leg straight, front knee bent.
Tuck pelvis under FIRST. Then lean forward.
Feel stretch in back hip. Hold 30s.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Pelvic tuck protects back.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Hip flexor length restoration — sitting shortens these.
whyForYou: Tight hip flexors tilt your pelvis and stress your back.
proTip: The pelvic tuck is EVERYTHING — without it you're stretching quads, not hip flexors.

name: Hip Abduction (Side-Lying)
steps: Side-lying. Bottom leg bent. Top leg straight.
Lift top leg 30° — lead with heel.
Hold 2s. Lower slowly.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Side-lying — no concern.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Glute med isolation for hip stability.
whyForYou: Glute med protects your knee and stabilizes your pelvis.
proTip: If you feel the front of the hip, you're rolling back.

name: Hip Adduction Stretch
steps: Seated butterfly — soles together, knees out.
Gently press knees toward floor with elbows.
Hold 30s.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Sit on block if can't sit tall.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Adductor flexibility for hip health.
whyForYou: Tight adductors contribute to knee valgus.
proTip: Sit on a yoga block if you can't keep spine upright.

name: Functional Hip Loading Progression
steps: Progress: bilateral squat → split squat → single-leg balance → step-ups.
Each level must be pain-free before progressing.
Track which level you're at.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Progressive — respect limits.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Final McKenzie hip step — functional loading.
whyForYou: When single-leg activities are pain-free, hip rehab is complete.
proTip: Film yourself from the front — watch for hip drop on single-leg work.

name: Ankle Dorsiflexion Mobilization
steps: Face wall. Front foot 4 inches from wall.
Rock knee forward — try to touch wall. Heel stays DOWN.
10 reps each side.

injuryNotes.lower_back: No concern.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: McKenzie Ankle Step 1 — dorsiflexion ROM.
whyForYou: Better ankle mobility = safer squats.
proTip: Measure distance from wall — track weekly.

name: Ankle Plantarflexion Stretch
steps: Sit with legs extended. Point toes fully.
Hold 15s. Pull toes back. Repeat.
Progress to kneeling sit-back for deeper stretch.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Seated — no concern.
injuryNotes.knee: Kneeling version requires knee flexion tolerance.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Plantarflexion ROM restoration.
whyForYou: Full ankle ROM in both directions prevents compensations.
proTip: If seated is easy, try kneeling sit-back for deeper stretch.

name: Ankle Inversion/Eversion ROM
steps: Seated. Rotate foot INWARD (inversion) — hold 5s.
Rotate foot OUTWARD (eversion) — hold 5s.
10 reps each direction.

injuryNotes.lower_back: No concern.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Frontal plane ankle ROM.
whyForYou: Inversion/eversion stability prevents ankle sprains.
proTip: Weak eversion is the most common ankle instability cause.

name: Toe Flexion/Extension ROM
steps: Barefoot. Lift all toes up. Lower.
Spread toes apart. Bring together.
Lift big toe only, then little toes only.
formCues: ✅ Try to control each toe independently

injuryNotes.lower_back: No concern.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Foot intrinsic control and arch function.
whyForYou: Strong foot intrinsics improve your foundation for everything.
proTip: Most people can't do this at first. 2 weeks of practice changes it.

name: Calf Eccentric Lowering
steps: Stand on step edge — both feet rise up.
Shift to ONE foot. Lower that heel SLOWLY below step — 3s.
Use both feet to push back up. Repeat.
formCues: ✅ Both feet up, one foot down

injuryNotes.lower_back: No concern.
injuryNotes.knee: Bent-knee version targets soleus. Straight knee = gastroc.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Hold rail for balance.

purpose: Eccentric calf strengthening — gold standard for tendon health.
whyForYou: Builds tendon resilience for all lower body work.
proTip: Mild discomfort OK per Alfredson protocol. Sharp pain = stop.

name: Single-Leg Balance Progression
steps: Single-leg stand — eyes open 30s.
Progress: eyes closed → unstable surface → reaching.
Track hold times.

injuryNotes.lower_back: No concern.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Ankle proprioception and stability progression.
whyForYou: Balance is the foundation for all athletic movement.
proTip: Eyes closed is 2x harder — that's the real test.

name: Functional Ankle Loading
steps: Progress: walking → fast walking → light jogging → direction changes.
Each level pain-free before progressing.
Add sport-specific movements last.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Progressive loading.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Final McKenzie ankle step — return to full function.
whyForYou: When you can jog and change direction pain-free, ankle rehab is complete.
proTip: Sport-specific drills are the LAST thing to add, not the first.

name: Child's Pose
steps: Kneel, sit hips back toward heels.
Walk hands forward, forehead to floor.
Breathe into lower back — 5 deep breaths.

injuryNotes.lower_back: One of the BEST positions for decompression.
injuryNotes.knee: Rolled towel behind knees if uncomfortable.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Arms at sides if overhead bothers shoulder.

purpose: Spinal decompression and parasympathetic activation.
whyForYou: Your back's reset button. Use daily.
proTip: This is a resting pose — stay as long as you want.

name: Cat-Cow Flow (Yoga)
steps: All fours. Cow: inhale, drop belly, lift chest.
Cat: exhale, round spine, tuck chin.
Flow with breath — one transition per breath.

breathing.pattern: Inhale to cow, exhale to cat

injuryNotes.lower_back: Gentle segmental motion. Reduce range if sharp pain.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Fists if wrists hurt.

purpose: Spinal segmental mobility with breath.
whyForYou: Warms every vertebral segment before training.
proTip: Close eyes — feel the wave through your spine.

name: Supine Twist
steps: Lie on back. Knees to chest.
Let both knees fall to one side.
Arms in T. Look opposite direction. Hold 30s.
Switch sides.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Gentle supported rotation. Stop if sharp pain.
injuryNotes.shoulder: T position — bend elbow if shoulder limited.

purpose: Gentle thoracic rotation and spinal release.
whyForYou: End-of-session spinal release. Safe and effective.
proTip: 5 slow breaths each side. This is medicine.

name: Legs Up the Wall
steps: Sit sideways against wall. Swing legs up.
Scoot hips to wall. Legs straight up.
Arms at sides or on belly. Breathe. 3-5 min.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Decompresses lumbar. Excellent for back.
injuryNotes.knee: Straight legs — slight bend if needed.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Arms wherever comfortable.

purpose: Circulation, gentle hamstring stretch, parasympathetic activation.
whyForYou: The single best restorative pose. Try it daily.
proTip: Set a timer for 5 min. Close your eyes. This changes everything.

name: Seated Forward Fold
steps: Sit with legs extended. Sit tall first.
Hinge from hips — fold forward.
Hold wherever you reach. 30s.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Hinge from hips. Stop if back pain.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Posterior chain stretch and calming.
whyForYou: Calming pose. Stretches hamstrings without back risk when done correctly.
proTip: Bend knees slightly if hamstrings are tight. The hip hinge matters more than reaching toes.

name: Bridge Pose
steps: Lie on back. Knees bent, feet hip-width.
Press through heels. Lift hips.
Hold at top — squeeze glutes. 30s.

injuryNotes.lower_back: No hyperextension. Glutes should drive, not back.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Gentle glute activation + hip flexor opening.
whyForYou: Opens hip flexors that tighten from sitting.
proTip: Place a block between knees to activate glute med simultaneously.

name: Reclined Butterfly
steps: Lie on back. Soles together, knees fall open.
Arms at sides. Breathe. 2-3 min.
Blocks under knees if too intense.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Supine — supported.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Passive hip opener and groin stretch.
whyForYou: Opens hips without any effort. Restorative.
proTip: Props make this sustainable for longer holds. Don't tough it out — use blocks.

name: Corpse Pose (Savasana)
steps: Lie flat. Arms at sides, palms up.
Close eyes. Scan body — release tension.
Breathe naturally. Stay 3-5 min.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Bolster under knees if needed.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Full body relaxation and nervous system reset.
whyForYou: The most important pose. Integration of everything you just did.
proTip: This is NOT optional. It's where the benefits integrate. Stay 5 min.

name: Knees to Chest
steps: Lie on back. Pull both knees to chest.
Wrap arms around shins.
Rock gently side to side. 30s.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Gentle lumbar flexion. Good for most backs.
injuryNotes.knee: Hands behind knees if knee is sensitive.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Lower back relief and gentle hip flexion.
whyForYou: Quick back relief pose. Do anytime your back feels tight.
proTip: The gentle rocking massages the sacrum and SI joint.

name: Thread the Needle
steps: All fours. Thread right arm under left — shoulder to floor.
Hold 5 breaths. Open right arm to ceiling.
Return. Repeat other side.

breathing.pattern: Exhale to thread, inhale to open

injuryNotes.lower_back: Thoracic rotation — protects lumbar.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Reduce depth if shoulder catches.

purpose: Thoracic rotation + shoulder stretch.
whyForYou: Opens your thoracic spine so your lumbar doesn't have to rotate.
proTip: The OPEN phase (arm to ceiling) is as important as the thread.

name: Downward Dog
steps: From plank, push hips UP and BACK.
Hands shoulder-width, feet hip-width.
Press heels toward floor. Hold 30s.
formCues: ✅ Straight spine — bend knees if needed

injuryNotes.lower_back: Bend knees to keep spine straight.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Weight-bearing — shoulder severity ≤3.

purpose: Full posterior chain stretch + shoulder stability.
whyForYou: The most complete single stretch. Works everything.
proTip: Bend your knees as much as needed — straight spine matters more than straight legs.

name: Warrior I
steps: Step one foot back. Front knee at 90°.
Back foot angled 45°. Square hips forward.
Arms overhead. Hold 30s each side.
commonMistakes: ❌ Back foot flat causing hip rotation

injuryNotes.lower_back: Keep ribs down — no arching.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Arms overhead — severity ≤3.

purpose: Hip flexor stretch + balance + leg endurance.
whyForYou: Functional lunge position with hip flexor opening.
proTip: The back hip flexor is where the stretch should be.

name: Warrior II
steps: Wide stance. Front foot forward, back foot perpendicular.
Front knee bends to 90°.
Arms wide at shoulder height. Gaze over front hand. Hold 30s.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Neutral spine.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Arms at shoulder height — manageable for most.

purpose: Hip opener + leg endurance + focus.
whyForYou: Builds leg endurance and hip stability in open position.
proTip: Sink lower for more challenge. Rise up when fatigued.

name: Triangle Pose
steps: Wide stance like Warrior II but front leg straight.
Hinge at hip — reach front hand to shin/floor.
Top arm reaches to ceiling. Hold 30s.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Hinge from hip — no spinal rounding.
injuryNotes.knee: Front leg straight but not locked.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Top arm up — modify if limited.

purpose: Lateral chain stretch — obliques, IT band, intercostals.
whyForYou: Stretches the lateral line that rarely gets attention.
proTip: Use a block under bottom hand — makes the pose accessible and safe.

name: Pigeon Pose (Modified with Props)
steps: From all fours, slide right knee forward.
Back leg extends straight. Block under front hip.
Fold forward as tolerated. Hold 60s each side.
formCues: ✅ Fold only as deep as comfortable

injuryNotes.lower_back: Fold only within comfort.
injuryNotes.knee: Front shin angle adjustable. Stop if knee pain.
injuryNotes.shoulder: No impact.

purpose: Deep hip external rotation stretch — modified for safety.
whyForYou: Opens the deep hip rotators that compress nerves when tight.
proTip: ALWAYS use a block. It keeps hips level and makes the pose sustainable.

name: Low Lunge
steps: Step one foot forward into deep lunge. Back knee on floor.
Sink hips down and forward.
Arms overhead or on front knee. Hold 30s.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Keep torso upright.
injuryNotes.knee: Back knee padded. Front knee over ankle.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Arms overhead optional.

purpose: Deep hip flexor stretch.
whyForYou: Opens the hip flexors shortened by sitting all day.
proTip: Add a gentle backbend at the top for extra hip flexor + ab stretch.

name: Cobra Pose
steps: Lie face down. Hands under shoulders.
Press up — lift chest. Keep hips on floor.
Hold 15-30s. Lower slowly.

breathing.pattern: Inhale to lift, hold, exhale to lower

injuryNotes.lower_back: Similar to McKenzie press-up. Gentle extension.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Moderate load. Severity ≤3.

purpose: Gentle spinal extension — similar to McKenzie press-up.
whyForYou: Extension opens the front of the body and benefits disc health.
proTip: Baby cobra (elbows bent) before full cobra. Always.

name: Chair Pose
steps: Stand feet together. Sit hips back — like sitting in a chair.
Arms overhead or at heart. Hold 30s.
Weight in heels. Knees over toes.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Sit back — don't arch.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Arms overhead optional.

purpose: Quad endurance + ankle mobility in a static hold.
whyForYou: Builds the leg endurance your sports demand.
proTip: The deeper you go, the harder it gets. Find YOUR depth.

name: Crow Pose
steps: Squat. Place hands on floor shoulder-width.
Lean forward. Place knees on backs of upper arms.
Shift weight forward — feet lift off floor. Hold.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Core must be strong.
injuryNotes.knee: Knees on arms — no direct stress.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Full weight on wrists/shoulders. Severity ≤2.

purpose: Upper body strength + core + balance integration.
whyForYou: An advanced milestone. Requires significant core and arm strength.
proTip: Place a pillow in front of your face. Removes the fear of falling.

name: Wheel Pose (Full Backbend)
steps: Lie on back. Hands by ears, fingers toward shoulders.
Press up into full backbend.
Hold 15-30s. Lower slowly.

injuryNotes.lower_back: BLOCKED if back severity ≥2. Full spinal extension.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Full overhead. Severity ≤1.

purpose: Full spinal extension — extreme mobility.
whyForYou: An advanced flexibility goal. Currently blocked by back severity.
proTip: Bridge pose first. Then bridge with head on floor. THEN wheel.

name: Headstand Prep (Wall Only)
steps: Forearms on floor, interlace fingers. Head in hands.
Walk feet in. Lift one leg at a time against wall.
Hold with wall support. Build toward freestanding.
formCues: ✅ Most weight on forearms, NOT head

injuryNotes.lower_back: Inversion — no compression.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Full overhead load. BLOCKED if neck or shoulder issues.

purpose: Inversion + core + shoulder stability.
whyForYou: Advanced goal. Requires neck and shoulder health.
proTip: 90% of weight on forearms. Head is just a balance point, not a weight-bearing surface.

name: Dancer's Pose
steps: Stand on one leg. Grab opposite ankle behind you.
Lean forward slightly. Kick foot into hand.
Reach forward arm. Hold 15-30s.
formCues: ✅ Kick into hand — creates the arch

injuryNotes.lower_back: Slight extension — manageable.
injuryNotes.shoulder: One arm overhead.

purpose: Balance + quad stretch + shoulder flexibility integration.
whyForYou: Beautiful integration of balance, flexibility, and control.
proTip: Start with hand against wall for balance support.

name: Side Plank Pose
steps: From plank, rotate to one side.
Stack feet or stagger. Top arm to ceiling.
Hold 15-30s each side.

injuryNotes.lower_back: Lateral core challenge.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Full load on bottom shoulder. Severity ≤3.

purpose: Oblique strength + shoulder stability in yoga context.
whyForYou: Combines lateral core work with shoulder stability.
proTip: Bottom knee on floor is the regression. Build up.

name: Depth Jump
steps: Set up in proper starting position
Execute movement with maximum intent
Land or decelerate with control
Reset and repeat
formCues: Drive through the ground with full force
Land softly with knees tracking toes
Full hip extension on each rep
coreBracing: HIGH — maintain neutral spine during all explosive movements.

breathing.pattern: Exhale on effort, inhale on reset

injuryNotes.lower_back: Avoid if acute back pain. Ensure hip hinge competency first.
injuryNotes.knee: No plyometrics if knee severity 3+. Ensure pain-free jumping.
injuryNotes.shoulder: Overhead throws contraindicated if shoulder at end-range.

purpose: Develops reactive power and sport-specific speed.
whyForYou: Part of your Performance Enhancement track.
proTip: Quality over quantity. Stop when speed drops.

name: Stability Ball Dumbbell Chest Press
steps: Sit on ball, walk feet forward until upper back/shoulders rest on ball
Hips up, forming bridge — core engaged throughout
Hold dumbbells at chest height, press up
Lower slowly (4 sec), pause (2 sec), press up (1 sec)
formCues: Hips level with shoulders — don't let them drop
Core braced — the ball creates instability demand
Control the dumbbells — don't let them drift
commonMistakes: Ball positioned too high (neck on ball instead of shoulders)
coreBracing: HIGH — 30-40% brace to maintain bridge on ball.
purpose: Combines chest pressing with core stabilization. The unstable surface recruits deep core muscles that a flat bench doesn't challenge.
proTip: Start lighter than your normal bench press — the stability demand reduces pressing strength ~20%.

name: Stability Ball Push-Up
steps: Place hands on sides of stability ball
Walk feet back to plank position
Lower chest toward ball with control
Press back up maintaining plank
formCues: Body in straight line from head to heels
commonMistakes: Hands too far forward on ball
purpose: The unstable surface forces shoulder stabilizers and core to work harder than floor push-ups.

name: Stability Ball Dumbbell Row
steps: Lie chest-down on stability ball, feet wide for balance
Hold dumbbells hanging below shoulders
Pull dumbbells up toward ribs, squeeze shoulder blades
Lower with control (4 sec)
formCues: Chest stays on ball — don't lift torso
purpose: Anti-extension core demand combined with rowing pattern. NASM Phase 1 stability pull.

name: Stability Ball Back Extension
steps: Lie face-down over ball, hips at ball apex
Feet anchored against wall or heavy object
Hands behind head or across chest
Extend spine upward, hold 2 seconds, lower slowly
purpose: Safe spinal extension strengthening with core demand from ball instability.

name: Stability Ball Hamstring Curl
steps: Lie on back, place heels on top of stability ball
Bridge hips up — straight line from shoulders to heels
Curl ball toward glutes by bending knees
Extend legs back out with control
formCues: Squeeze glutes at top of bridge
Don't let ball drift side to side
purpose: Hamstring strengthening with massive core stabilization demand. NASM Phase 1 staple.

name: Stability Ball Russian Twist
steps: Sit on ball, walk feet forward until shoulders rest on ball
Bridge hips up, arms extended straight up toward ceiling
Rotate torso to one side — arms stay straight, ball rolls under shoulders
Return to center, rotate opposite side
purpose: Rotational core with unstable base. Progress by holding a dumbbell or medicine ball.

name: Stability Ball Plank
steps: Place forearms on stability ball
Walk feet back to plank position
Hold — body straight from head to heels
Focus on not letting ball drift
purpose: Harder than floor plank — the ball adds instability that fires deep core stabilizers.

name: Stability Ball Y-T-W Raises
steps: Lie chest-down on stability ball
Y: raise arms overhead in Y shape, thumbs up — hold 2s
T: raise arms out to sides in T shape — hold 2s
W: bend elbows, pull into W shape, squeeze shoulder blades — hold 2s
purpose: Scapular stabilizer strengthening in all 3 positions. Essential for shoulder health.

name: Stability Ball Dumbbell Fly
steps: Bridge on ball as for chest press
Hold dumbbells above chest, slight elbow bend
Lower arms out to sides in arc (4 sec)
Bring back together, squeezing chest
purpose: Chest isolation with stability ball core demand. Stretched position loads the pec.

name: Stability Ball Overhead Tricep Extension
steps: Sit upright on stability ball, feet flat
Hold single dumbbell overhead with both hands
Lower behind head bending elbows
Extend back up, lock out
purpose: Tricep isolation with unstable seated position challenging core balance.

name: Stability Ball Reverse Hyperextension
steps: Lie face-down on ball, hands on floor in front
Legs hanging behind, toes on floor
Lift both legs up, squeezing glutes at top
Lower with control
purpose: Glute and lower back strengthening in a spine-neutral position.

name: BOSU Push-Up
steps: Place hands on edges of BOSU dome (flat side up or down)
Walk feet back to plank position
Lower chest toward BOSU
Press back up with control
purpose: Unstable push-up surface increases shoulder stabilizer and core recruitment.

name: BOSU Reverse Lunge
steps: Stand on BOSU dome with both feet
Step one foot back off the BOSU into reverse lunge
Lower until front thigh is parallel
Drive through front heel to return to standing on BOSU
purpose: Single-leg balance on unstable surface + lunge strength. NASM Phase 1 progression.

name: BOSU Plank
steps: Place forearms on BOSU dome
Walk feet back to plank position
Hold — body straight, don't let BOSU tilt
purpose: Unstable plank surface increases core demand beyond floor plank.

name: BOSU Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
steps: Stand on BOSU dome on one leg
Hinge forward at hips, extending rear leg behind
Reach toward ground, maintaining flat back
Return to standing with control
purpose: Maximum proprioceptive demand — single-leg hinge on unstable surface.

name: Single-Leg Squat to Box
steps: Stand on one leg in front of a box/bench
Sit back and down to touch the box lightly
Drive through heel to stand back up
Don't put weight on box — just touch and go
purpose: Unilateral strength and balance. The box limits depth for safety while building single-leg control.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift (Bodyweight)
steps: Stand on one leg, slight knee bend
Hinge forward from hips, extending rear leg behind
Reach toward ground — back stays flat
Return to standing with control
purpose: Posterior chain + balance in one movement. Foundational NASM single-leg hinge.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift (Dumbbell)
steps: Hold dumbbell in one or both hands
Stand on one leg, slight knee bend
Hinge forward, dumbbell tracks along standing leg
Return to standing, squeezing glute
purpose: Loaded single-leg hinge — builds posterior chain strength with balance demand.

name: Single-Leg Cable Row
steps: Stand on one leg facing cable machine
Grip handle with opposite hand
Pull handle to ribs, squeeze shoulder blade back
Return with control — maintain single-leg balance
purpose: NASM Phase 2 staple — combines pulling strength with single-leg proprioception.

name: Single-Leg Dumbbell Shoulder Press
steps: Stand on one leg, hold dumbbell at shoulder height
Press dumbbell overhead while maintaining balance
Lower with control
Complete all reps, switch legs
purpose: Overhead pressing with single-leg balance — NASM Phase 2 superset partner.

name: Single-Leg Dumbbell Curl
steps: Stand on one leg, hold dumbbell in opposite hand
Curl dumbbell up with controlled bicep contraction
Lower with 4-second eccentric
Maintain balance throughout
purpose: Bicep isolation with balance challenge. NASM proprioceptive enrichment principle.

name: Barbell Bench Press
steps: Lie on bench, feet flat on floor
Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder width
Unrack, lower to mid-chest (3 sec)
Press up to lockout
formCues: Retract shoulder blades — squeeze them together
Slight arch in upper back, not lower back
Bar path: slight diagonal from mid-chest to over shoulders
purpose: Gold standard horizontal push. Primary chest mass builder.

name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
steps: Set bench to 30-45 degree incline
Grip bar slightly wider than shoulders
Lower to upper chest
Press to lockout
purpose: Targets upper chest (clavicular head) — key for full chest development.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
steps: Grip bar at shoulder width, bar in front rack
Brace core, press bar overhead
Lock out with bar directly over midfoot
Lower to front rack with control
formCues: Push head through once bar passes forehead
Core braced — no excessive lean back
commonMistakes: Pressing in front instead of straight up
purpose: Primary vertical push compound. Builds shoulder mass and overhead pressing strength.

name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
steps: Hinge at hips, torso ~45 degrees
Grip bar overhand, slightly wider than shoulder
Pull bar to lower chest/upper abdomen
Lower with control, full arm extension
formCues: Flat back — chest up, shoulders back
Pull elbows back, not just up
Initiate with shoulder blades, finish with arms
purpose: Primary horizontal pull compound. Builds back thickness and width.

name: T-Bar Row
steps: Straddle bar, hinge at hips
Grip handle or V-handle attached to bar
Pull to chest, squeeze shoulder blades
Lower with control
purpose: Neutral grip rowing variation — easy on wrists, great for back thickness.

name: Arnold Press
steps: Start with dumbbells at chin height, palms facing you (curl position)
Press up while rotating palms to face forward
Full lockout overhead, palms forward
Reverse the rotation as you lower
purpose: Hits all three deltoid heads in one movement. Arnold's signature shoulder exercise.

name: Pike Push-Up
steps: Start in downward dog position — hips high, hands shoulder width
Bend elbows, lower head toward floor between hands
Press back up to start position
Keep hips high throughout — this targets shoulders not chest
purpose: Bodyweight vertical push — progression toward handstand push-up.

name: Chin-Up
steps: Grip bar underhand (supinated), shoulder width
Hang with full arm extension
Pull chin over bar, leading with chest
Lower with control to full hang
purpose: Supinated pull-up — more bicep involvement than overhand. Often easier as first pull-up progression.

name: Cable Fly (Low to High)
steps: Set cables at lowest position
Grip handles, step forward
Arc arms up and together in front of upper chest
Lower with control, feel chest stretch
purpose: Targets upper chest from low angle. Constant cable tension throughout ROM.

name: Cable Fly (High to Low)
steps: Set cables at highest position
Grip handles, step forward
Arc arms down and together in front of lower chest
Return with control
purpose: Targets lower/sternal chest from high angle.

name: Cable Pull-Through
steps: Face away from cable, rope between legs
Hinge at hips, letting cable pull you back
Drive hips forward, squeezing glutes at top
Control the return
purpose: Hip hinge pattern teacher — great for learning to load the glutes before deadlifts.

name: Pistol Squat (Assisted)
steps: Stand on one leg, other leg extended in front
Sit back and down as far as controlled
Drive through heel to stand back up
Use TRX strap or doorframe for assistance if needed
purpose: Ultimate single-leg squat — requires mobility, strength, and balance.

name: Overhead Carry
steps: Press dumbbell or kettlebell overhead, lock out arm
Walk forward with controlled steps
Keep arm vertical — bicep by ear
Maintain neutral spine, don't lean
purpose: Overhead stability + core anti-lateral flexion + shoulder endurance.

name: Renegade Row
steps: Plank position with hands on dumbbells
Row one dumbbell to hip while maintaining plank
Lower with control, row other side
Resist rotation — hips stay square to floor
purpose: Anti-rotation core + pulling strength. Plank that fights rotation every rep.

name: Cable Woodchop (High to Low)
steps: Set cable at highest position
Stand sideways to cable, grip handle with both hands
Pull diagonally down and across body, rotating torso
Control return — resist the cable pulling you back
purpose: Rotational core strength — NASM Phase 2-3 core strength exercise.

name: Cable Reverse Woodchop (Low to High)
steps: Set cable at lowest position
Stand sideways, grip handle with both hands at hip
Lift diagonally up and across body, rotating torso
Control return
purpose: Rotational lift pattern — ACE IFT multiplanar movement.

name: Tuck Jump
steps: Stand with feet shoulder width
Jump explosively, pulling knees to chest at peak
Land softly with bent knees
Immediately repeat
purpose: Intermediate plyometric — develops explosive leg power and hip flexor speed.

name: Broad Jump
steps: Stand with feet shoulder width, squat down slightly
Swing arms back, then explosively forward
Jump for maximum distance
Land softly, absorb with bent knees
purpose: Horizontal power production — measures and develops explosive hip extension.

name: Spiderman Lunge with Rotation
steps: Step forward into deep lunge
Place same-side hand on floor inside front foot
Rotate opposite arm toward ceiling, opening chest
Hold 2-3 seconds, return, step forward and repeat other side
purpose: Hip flexor + adductor + thoracic rotation in one movement. NASM CEx integration.

name: Inchworm
steps: Stand tall, hinge at hips, touch floor
Walk hands forward to plank position
Perform one push-up (optional)
Walk feet toward hands, stand up, repeat
purpose: Dynamic hamstring + shoulder + core integration. Full-body warm-up in one movement.

name: Squat to Stand
steps: Stand with feet shoulder width
Hinge and grab toes
Pull yourself into deep squat, chest up
Stand up by extending legs while holding toes, then release
purpose: Hip mobility + hamstring flexibility + squat patterning in one flow.

name: Lunge with Elbow to Instep
steps: Step into deep forward lunge
Place both hands inside front foot
Drop same-side elbow toward instep of front foot
Hold 2-3 seconds, extend front leg for hamstring stretch, repeat
purpose: NASM-specific warm-up combining hip, ankle, and thoracic mobility.

name: Transverse Plane Lunge with Reach
steps: Stand tall, step and rotate into transverse lunge (open hip)
Reach arms in direction of rotation
Push off rotated foot to return to start
Alternate sides
purpose: ACE IFT multiplanar lunge — trains the often-neglected transverse plane.

name: Half-Kneeling Cable Chop
steps: Half-kneeling position sideways to cable (inside knee down)
Grip handle at shoulder height with both hands
Pull and chop diagonally across body toward opposite hip
Control return — resist rotation
purpose: ACE-specific anti-rotation + rotation in half-kneeling for hip stability.

name: Tall-Kneeling Pallof Press
steps: Kneel tall sideways to cable, handle at chest
Press handle straight out in front of chest
Hold 2-3 seconds — resist the cable rotating you
Return to chest and repeat
purpose: ACE IFT progression of Pallof press — tall kneeling removes ankle/knee compensation.
